title: Davallia repens
taxonomy: Davallia repens is a type of fern. It belongs to the family Davalliaceae. This family is a part of the order Polypodiales, which is a group of vascular plants. The order Polypodiales is further grouped under the division Pteridophyta, which includes all types of ferns. The classification of Davallia repens places it as a vascular plant with a specific evolutionary position.
uses: Davallia repens is a type of plant with various traditional uses, including its application in medicine. The leaves of Davallia repens have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues. Its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. However, more research is required to confirm its efficacy in treating specific conditions. Further studies are needed to fully understand the potential benefits of Davallia repens in medicinal contexts.

parts: Davallia repens is a plant species with a history of traditional medicinal use, particularly in the regions of Asia and Australia. The rhizomes and leaves of Davallia repens are used in various herbal remedies to treat several health conditions. The rhizomes are used to treat fever, while the leaves are used to treat skin irritations and inflammation. In some traditional medicine practices, the plant is also used to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and bronchitis. The exact mechanisms of action of the plant's medicinal parts are not fully understood and require further research.
preparations: Davallia repens is a species of fern native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia, and its leaves and stems have been used in herbal preparations. The leaves of Davallia repens are rich in flavonoids, saponins, and other bioactive compounds that contribute to its medicinal properties. side_effects: Davallia repens is a type of fern, and there is limited information on its side effects due to its primarily ornamental use.
